item.title: EOC Midwives: Conference on Physiological Birth in Mendrisio
item.description: On May 6, 2026, EOC is organizing a public conference on midwifery care at Mendrisio Hospital. Focus on physiological birth and continuity of care.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Midwifery conference EOC on May 6, 2026 in Mendrisio
- Focus on physiological birth and continuity of care
- Entertainment area for children aged 4-10

## Key facts
- **What**: Public conference on midwifery care
- When: May 6, 2026, 16:30-19:00
- Where: Ospedale Beata Vergine, Mendrisio
- Who: Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC)
- Cost: Not specified

On May 6, 2026, the Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) will organize a public conference on midwifery care at the Ospedale Beata Vergine in Mendrisio. The event, on the occasion of International Midwife Day, will take place from 16:30 to 19:00 in the auditorium located on the -1 floor. The initiative aims to promote and enhance midwifery care throughout the birth process, with a particular focus on physiological birth.

During the conference, the team of specialists from the Women and Children's Department will present models of care that promote physiological birth. The event, moderated by PD Dr. med. Andrea Braga, is aimed at the public and all professional figures involved in the birth process. The goal is to foster a constructive exchange on best practices in midwifery care.

To allow parents with children to participate, from 17:00 to 19:00 there will be a supervised entertainment area for children aged 4 to 10. The young guests will also be offered a healthy snack.

### Detailed program

The conference program includes several thematic sessions:

- 16:30: Welcome
- 17:00: Welcome by PD Dr. med. Andrea Braga and Isabella Guarisco
- 17:10: The midwife and her office; perspectives and benefits for women, with Esther Voce Heine, Valentina Magni, Giada Rampini
- 17:30: Discussion
- 17:40: The perineum among the protagonists of childbirth; indications, advantages, clinical experience and best practices, with Margaretha Luethi and Alessandra Turcatti
- 18:00: Discussion
- 18:10: Promoting physiological birth; the role of the midwife, with Laura Loiodice and Silvia Holzknecht
- 18:30: Discussion
- 18:40: The 1000 days of birth; postpartum continuity of care, early and informed support, with Gilda Gobbi and Stephanie Todaro
- 19:00: Discussion
- 19:10: Refreshments

The children's entertainment area will be available from 17:00 to 19:00, only by registration via email to comunicazione.mendrisio@eoc.ch. The conference organized by EOC represents a unique opportunity to delve into the crucial role of midwives in the birth process. Physiological birth, which refers to natural childbirth without medical interventions, is at the center of attention. This approach aims to minimize unnecessary medical interventions, promoting a more natural and respectful birth experience for both maternal and fetal physiology.

The exchange between professionals and the public is fundamental to continuously improve obstetric practices. The discussions planned during the conference will allow for sharing experiences and knowledge, contributing to a constant improvement in the quality of obstetric care.

### The importance of continuity of care

Another central theme of the conference is postpartum continuity of care. The first 1000 days of a child's life are crucial for their development and well-being. Postpartum continuity of care ensures that the mother and child receive the necessary support during this delicate period. This includes emotional support, health education, and monitoring of both the mother's and child's health.

### Practical implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, the conference represents an opportunity to better understand the Swiss healthcare system and local obstetric practices. Participation in such events can help reduce linguistic and cultural barriers, facilitating access to quality healthcare services.

Furthermore, the conference offers the opportunity to learn about the resources available for families, such as children's play areas during public events. This can be particularly useful for cross-border workers seeking to balance work and family responsibilities.

### Conclusion

The EOC's conference on obstetric care in Mendrisio is a significant event for the community. It offers a platform for dialogue and continuous improvement of obstetric practices, while promoting the health and well-being of mothers and children. For cross-border workers, it represents an opportunity to better integrate into the Swiss healthcare system and access useful resources for their families.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). To participate in the obstetric care conference organized by EOC in Mendrisio, you need to follow a few simple steps. First, make sure to register your children for the entertainment area, if necessary. You can do this by sending an email to comunicazione.mendrisio@eoc.ch. Remember to provide all the required information to ensure a safe and pleasant experience for your little ones.

### Registration Procedure

1. **Send an email**: Contact comunicazione.mendrisio@eoc.ch to register your children for the entertainment area.
2. Provide the necessary information: Make sure to include your children's names and ages, as well as any other required information.
3. Confirm your participation: You will receive a confirmation of your registration and all the necessary information for the day of the event.

### What to Bring With You

- Identification documents: Bring a valid ID for yourself and your children.
- Contact information: Make sure you have your phone number and email address handy.
- Comfortable clothing: Wear comfortable clothes for you and your children, so you can actively participate in the sessions and enjoy the entertainment area.

### Use Our Healthcare Cost Calculator

To best plan your participation in the conference, you can use our [healthcare cost calculator](nav:health). This tool will help you estimate the costs associated with obstetric care and other healthcare expenses, ensuring you are prepared for any eventuality.

### Conclusion

The obstetric care conference organized by EOC in Mendrisio is a must-attend event for anyone interested in maternal and child well-being. Follow the steps above to ensure you participate and make the most of all the available resources. Don't forget to use our healthcare cost calculator to best plan your participation and ensure a positive experience for you and your family.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921155/la-nascita-e-il-ruolo-dell-ostetrica-accanto-alla-donna-ne-parla-l-eoc)*

item.title: Yellow alert for thunderstorms across the province of Varese
item.description: The Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region has issued a yellow weather alert for thunderstorms across the entire province of Varese, with risks of damage and hydraulic criticalities.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Yellow alert for thunderstorms in Varese
- Risks of damage to roofs and structures
- Possible localized hydrogeological instability

## Key facts
- **What**: Yellow weather alert for thunderstorms
- When: April 27, 2026, from 12:00
- Where: Province of Varese
- Who: Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region
- Amount: Not specified

The Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region issued a weather alert today, Monday, April 27, 2026, signaling a yellow-level thunderstorm risk in part of the province of Varese. The Varesotto territory is divided into two homogeneous alert zones with different scenarios. The Lakes and Varesine Prealps area is subject to the yellow alert for thunderstorms already from 12 noon today, expiring at 14:00 tomorrow, Tuesday, April 28. The area of the lower province, which also includes part of the province of Varese together with Milan, Monza Brianza, Como, and Lecco, is instead subject to a yellow alert for thunderstorms starting from midnight tonight until 14:00 tomorrow.

For both areas, the hydrogeological, hydraulic, and strong wind risks remain at the green level for the moment. On the meteorological level, in the afternoon and evening of today, scattered showers or thunderstorms are possible in the northern sectors, even of moderate intensity, with possible hail of small and medium size. During the day tomorrow, the widespread precipitation, expected during the night and in the morning, is forecast to gradually decrease in the afternoon.

### Risks and precautions

Civil Protection asks local systems to maintain an operational phase of Attention, preparing for the prompt activation of monitoring actions. Among the risks reported: damage to roofs and structures, falling trees, problems with traffic and overhead service networks, possible localized hydrogeological instability, and critical issues in the hydraulic network and urban drainage systems.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in the province of Varese, it is important to be aware of the risks related to thunderstorms. In case of bad weather, it is advisable to avoid unnecessary movements and constantly monitor weather forecasts. Moreover, it is fundamental to ensure that one's homes and vehicles are prepared for possible adverse weather conditions.

### Practical advice
- Constant monitoring: Use weather forecast apps and websites for real-time updates.
- Preparation of homes: Ensure that roofs, windows, and doors are well closed and that there are no objects that could be carried away by the wind.
- Traffic: Avoid driving during thunderstorms, especially in areas prone to flooding or landslides.
- Emergencies: Have an emergency kit with water, food, flashlight, and essential medications at hand. ## Analysis of Critical Issues

The yellow alert issued by the Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region for the province of Varese represents an important signal for the population and, in particular, for cross-border workers who daily move between Ticino and Lombardy. The forecasted thunderstorms can have a significant impact on traffic and the safety of infrastructure, making specific precautions necessary.

### Impact on Traffic

One of the main risks reported is represented by traffic issues. Intense thunderstorms can cause flooding, landslides, and falling trees, making roads and highways dangerous. For cross-border workers who daily use the connecting roads between Ticino and the province of Varese, it is fundamental to plan movements in advance and consider possible alternatives in case of road closures or slowdowns.

### Hydraulic and Hydrogeological Criticalities

Intense precipitation can also cause hydraulic and hydrogeological criticalities, such as floods and landslides. Urban drainage networks might not be sufficient to manage the volume of water, increasing the risk of flooding in urban areas. It is therefore important that residents and cross-border workers verify the conditions of their homes and be ready to intervene in case of emergency.

### Preparation of Homes

To minimize the damage caused by thunderstorms, it is advisable to adopt some preventive measures. For example, ensure that gutters are clean and functional, that windows and doors are well sealed, and that there are no objects outside that could be carried away by the wind. Additionally, it is useful to have an emergency kit with water, food, flashlight, and essential medications.

### Constant Monitoring

Another crucial aspect is the constant monitoring of weather forecasts. Using reliable weather forecast apps and websites can provide real-time updates on weather conditions and allow for informed decisions. In case of a weather alert, it is important to follow the indications of local authorities and stay in contact with emergency services.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino, it is fundamental to be prepared for possible disruptions in traffic or closures of border crossings. Planning movements in advance and considering possible alternatives can help avoid delays and ensure safety during the journey. Additionally, it is important to be aware of the risks related to thunderstorms and adopt the necessary measures to protect homes and vehicles. ## What to do in case of weather alert

In case of a yellow weather alert for thunderstorms, it is important to follow some guidelines to ensure your safety and that of your family. Here is a series of steps to follow:

1. **Monitor weather forecasts**: Use reliable weather forecast apps and websites for real-time updates on weather conditions.
2. Plan your trips: Avoid unnecessary travel and, if possible, postpone trips until weather conditions improve.
3. Prepare your home: Ensure that roofs, windows, and doors are well closed and that there are no objects outside that could be carried away by the wind.
4. Have an emergency kit: Prepare an emergency kit with water, food, flashlight, essential medications, and other necessities.
5. Follow the authorities' instructions: In case of a weather alert, follow the instructions of local authorities and stay in contact with emergency services.
6. Check road conditions: Before setting off, check road conditions and consider alternatives in case of closures or delays.
7. Protect your vehicles: Ensure that vehicles are parked in safe places and are not exposed to risks of flooding or falling trees.
8. Inform your family: In case of travel, inform your family of your itinerary and expected arrival.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to constantly monitor weather conditions and plan their trips, tools such as the [weather conditions calculator](nav:calculator) and the [weather forecast comparator](nav:exchange) can be useful. These tools can provide detailed information on weather conditions and help plan trips safely and efficiently.

### Conclusion

The yellow weather alert for thunderstorms across the province of Varese is an important signal for the population, and in particular for cross-border workers who daily travel between Ticino and Lombardy. By following the guidelines and adopting the necessary preventive measures, it is possible to minimize risks and ensure your safety during thunderstorms. Using tools such as the [weather conditions calculator](nav:calculator) and the [weather forecast comparator](nav:exchange) can help plan trips safely and efficiently.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/allerta-gialla-per-temporali-su-tutta-la-provincia-di-varese/2559307/)*

item.title: Unemployment for cross-border workers: Switzerland won't pay, costs up to one billion
item.description: Switzerland rejects the EU's proposal to pay unemployment benefits to cross-border workers, with costs up to one billion francs per year.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Switzerland will not pay unemployment benefits to cross-border workers
- Estimated costs between hundreds of millions and one billion francs
- Ticino should strengthen employment offices

## Key facts
- **What**: Payment of unemployment benefits to cross-border workers
- When: 2026 (EU agreement not yet ratified)
- Where: Switzerland, particularly Canton Ticino
- Who: Swiss Federal Council, EU, Lega dei Ticinesi
- Amount: Between hundreds of millions and one billion francs per year

Switzerland does not intend to pay unemployment benefits to cross-border workers, despite an internal EU agreement that would introduce this change. The news, anticipated by Aargauer Zeitung, has sparked immediate reactions in Ticino, where the Lega dei Ticinesi has presented a motion to block this possibility. National Councilor Lorenzo Quadri reiterated the Lega's position, asking the Federal Council to clarify to the EU that Switzerland does not intend to comply with this new rule.

The change in rules, if approved, would heavily impact Switzerland, which employs approximately 412,000 cross-border workers with a G permit. According to the SECO, cited by Aargauer Zeitung, the cost for Swiss unemployment insurance could range from several hundred million francs to one billion per year. Furthermore, cantons with a high number of cross-border workers, such as Ticino, would need to strengthen the regional employment offices to manage a potential increase in unemployment claims.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The change, if implemented, would have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Switzerland but reside in Italy. Currently, unemployment benefits are paid by the state of residence, with a contribution from the country where the cross-border worker carried out their work. The new rule would instead require the state of employment, i.e., Switzerland, to pay the benefits.

### Political reactions

The Lega dei Ticinesi immediately reacted to the news, presenting a motion to block this possibility. National Councilor Lorenzo Quadri stated that the Federal Council must clarify to the EU that Switzerland does not intend to comply with this new rule. The motion asks the Federal Council to communicate to the EU, without waiting for discussions in the Joint Committee, that it does not intend to comply with the new regulation on the payment of unemployment benefits to cross-border workers.

### Consequences for Ticino

The Canton of Ticino, with a high number of cross-border workers, would be particularly affected by this change. The regional employment offices would need to hire additional resources to manage a potential increase in unemployment claims, to the detriment of unemployed Ticinese. This situation contrasts with the 'light indigenous preference,' a policy that favors local employment. ### Analysis of Practical Implications

The proposal to have Switzerland pay unemployment benefits for cross-border workers raises several practical issues. First, Switzerland has always maintained a clear position: unemployment benefits are the competence of the country of residence, not the country of work. This principle has been reiterated several times by the Federal Council, which has stated that any change would require an amendment to Annex II of the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons, with the consent of Switzerland.

### Comparison with the Current Situation

Currently, cross-border workers who lose their jobs receive unemployment benefits from the country of residence, with a contribution from the country where they worked. This system was established to ensure that costs are fairly distributed between the two countries. The new rule, however, would shift the burden entirely onto Switzerland, which would have to manage a significant increase in spending on unemployment insurance.

### Possible Scenarios

If the EU agreement were ratified and Switzerland decided to comply, cantons with a high number of cross-border workers, such as Ticino, would face several challenges. The regional employment offices would need to be strengthened to handle a potential increase in unemployment claims. This could involve hiring new staff and allocating additional resources, at the expense of local unemployed people. Furthermore, the situation would contrast with the 'light indigenous preference', a policy aimed at promoting local employment.

### Economic Impact

The economic impact of this change would be significant. According to the SECO, spending on unemployment insurance could range from several hundred million francs to one billion per year. This additional cost could have repercussions on the federal budget and the cantons, which would need to find the necessary resources to manage the increase in unemployment claims.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ### What to do if you're a cross-border worker

If you're a cross-border worker employed in Switzerland and residing in Italy, it's important to monitor the developments of this situation. Currently, there are no immediate changes, but it's possible that the situation may evolve in the coming months. If the EU agreement is ratified and Switzerland decides to comply, cross-border workers may have to face new procedures to obtain unemployment benefits.

### Current procedure

Currently, cross-border workers who lose their jobs must contact INPS in Italy to apply for unemployment benefits. The procedure involves submitting a series of documents, including the unemployment certificate issued by the Swiss employer. It's important to keep all documents related to the employment relationship, including contracts and pay slips.

### Useful tools

To stay updated on the latest news and to get support in managing unemployment procedures, you can use the [unemployment calculator](nav:unemployment) available on the site. This tool allows you to estimate the amount of unemployment benefits and to better understand the procedures to follow.

### Conclusions

The current situation is evolving and may change in the coming months. It's important to stay informed and monitor developments to be prepared for any changes. If you have questions or need support, don't hesitate to contact the competent offices or use the tools available on the site.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/la-svizzera-non-paghi-la-disoccupazione-dei-frontalieri-le-cifre-fino-a-un-miliardo-di-franchi/)*

item.title: Up to 500 francs fine for vaping in train stations
item.description: The Swiss Association for Tobacco Prevention calls for fines for those who violate the ban on smoking and vaping on the tracks of Swiss train stations
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Swiss Association for Tobacco Prevention proposes fines of up to 500 francs for vaping in stations
- SBB receives between three and four reports per week for smoking on the tracks
- Director Luciano Ruggia asks for improved signage for vaping areas

## Key facts
- **What**: Proposal for fines for violating the smoking and vaping ban
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Swiss railway stations
- Who: Swiss Association for Tobacco Prevention
- Amount: Up to 500 francs

The Swiss Association for Tobacco Prevention has proposed introducing fines of up to 500 francs for those who violate the smoking and vaping ban on the tracks of Swiss stations. Currently, smoking is allowed within a radius of two meters from the designated ashtrays, but the use of electronic cigarettes is not always respected. Many vapers release vapor into the crowd, causing discontent among other users.

The director of the association, Luciano Ruggia, stated that a fine of 500 francs would be reasonable and justified. He also emphasized the need to improve signage, which currently does not include devices for vaping. Ruggia proposed introducing ground markings in areas where smoking is allowed to make the ban clearer.

Swiss Federal Railways (SBB) explained that currently no fines are issued for smoking and vaping on the tracks, but failure to comply with the regulations can result in consequences such as removal, compensation requests, or legal proceedings. Additionally, administrative costs of at least 25 francs can be charged. Moritz Weisskopf, spokesperson for SBB, assured that the vast majority of smokers respect the rules.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who use Swiss railway stations to travel between the Canton of Ticino and Italy could be affected by this proposal. It is important that they are aware of the rules and possible sanctions to avoid fines or other consequences. Furthermore, the proposal to improve signage could make it clearer where smoking and vaping are allowed, facilitating compliance with the rules.

SBB receives between three and four reports per week from customers complaining about smoking in the stations. This indicates that the problem is widespread and that current measures are not sufficient to ensure a smoke- and vapor-free environment. The proposal to introduce fines could be a step towards greater compliance with the rules. ### Analysis of Practical Implications

The proposal to introduce fines for smoking and vaping on the platforms of Swiss stations could have several practical implications for travelers, including cross-border workers who regularly use railway stations. First, it is important that travelers are aware of the rules and possible sanctions. Fines of up to 500 francs could represent a significant deterrent for those who do not comply with the smoking and vaping ban.

Furthermore, the proposal to improve signage could make it clearer where smoking and vaping are allowed. Currently, the signs only depict traditional cigarettes and not vaping devices. The introduction of ground markings in areas where smoking is allowed could help reduce violations of the ban.

### Comparisons with the Current Situation

Currently, the FFS do not issue fines for smoking and vaping on the platforms, but failure to comply with the regulations can result in consequences such as removal, requests for compensation, or legal proceedings. Additionally, administrative costs of at least 25 francs can be charged. The proposal to introduce fines of up to 500 francs would represent a significant change from the current situation.

### Future Scenarios

If the proposal is approved, travelers who smoke or vape on the platforms of Swiss stations could be subject to fines of up to 500 francs. This could lead to greater compliance with the rules and a more smoke-free and vapor-free environment in the stations. However, it is important that the FFS and the competent authorities ensure that the signage is clear and that the fines are applied fairly.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do if you are fined

If you are fined for smoking or vaping on the tracks of Swiss stations, it is important to follow the procedures indicated by the SBB. First of all, you need to pay the fine within the set deadline. If you believe the fine is unjust, you can file an appeal. The SBB provides detailed information on how to file an appeal and what to do in case of dispute.

### Procedure to file an appeal

1. **Fine receipt**: Keep the fine receipt, which contains important information such as the reference number and the payment deadline.
2. Appeal form: Fill out the appeal form available on the SBB website or at railway stations.
3. Documentation: Attach all relevant documentation to the appeal application, such as photographs, testimonies, or other evidence that may support your position.
4. Submission of the appeal: Send the completed appeal form and the attached documentation to the address indicated by the SBB.
5. Waiting for the response: Wait for the SBB's response, which may request further information or documentation.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to know more about the rules and possible penalties for smoking and vaping in Swiss stations, you can use the [fine calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool can help you calculate the fine amount based on specific circumstances and better understand the consequences of not respecting the rules.

Additionally, you can consult the practical guides to obtain detailed information on the procedures to follow in case of a fine and how to file an appeal. These guides provide useful tips and step-by-step instructions for effectively addressing the situation.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921141/sigarette-fumo-fumatori-divieto-associazione)*

item.title: Dry April: drought alert in Ticino and Switzerland
item.description: MeteoSwiss issues a warning: April 2026 is the driest on record, with severe rainfall shortages in Ticino and other regions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- April 2026 is the driest since records began
- Only 15% of average rainfall in Ticino
- Marked risk of forest fires

## Key facts
- **What**: Exceptional drought
- When: April 2026
- Where: Ticino, Altopiano, Valais
- Who: MeteoSwiss
- Amount: 10 mm of rain recorded

Switzerland is experiencing an exceptionally dry April, according to the national weather service. According to the Federal Office of Meteorology and Climatology (MeteoSwiss), this April is set to be the driest since records began. As of April 23, only about a third of the average precipitation for the reference period 1991-2020 had fallen nationwide. In some areas, the shortage is much more severe: the western Altopiano region, the canton of Valais and some areas of the canton of Ticino have received just over 15% of their usual April rains. Many weather stations have recorded just 10 millimetres of rain since the start of the month. Under normal conditions, the Altopiano usually receives between 70 and 100 millimetres in April, while the southern side of the Alps records between 100 and 200.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The prolonged absence of rain has also increased the risk of forest fires. The danger level is considered marked to high in much of the canton of Grisons and marked in Ticino. Several Grisons municipalities have already imposed bans on lighting fires. Water reserves in dams have also been affected. According to the Federal Office of Energy, the basins are currently full on average by 12% at national level, a figure well below seasonal norms. Levels stand at 9% in Ticino, 8% in Valais and 11% in Grisons. The agricultural world is also expressing concern, although the situation is not yet considered critical, said the Swiss Farmers' Union. For now, it is not expected that the lack of rain will affect crops.

### Comparison with seasonal averages
MeteoSwiss forecasts do not indicate significant rainfall and the drought index is set to remain high until the beginning of May. A change in the situation is expected during the first half of next month. Significant rains occurred only on 12 and 13 April. The current situation represents a significant anomaly compared to seasonal averages, with potential consequences for agriculture, water resource management and forest fire prevention. - ## Analysis of the consequences
The exceptional drought of April 2026 is having a significant impact on various sectors in Ticino and other Swiss regions. The increased risk of forest fires has led to the imposition of bans on lighting fires in several municipalities, particularly in the Grisons. This type of restriction can have a direct impact on the daily lives of cross-border workers, who must be aware of local regulations and safety measures.

### Impact on water reserves
The water reserves of the dams are currently below seasonal norms. In Ticino, the basins are 9% full, a worrying figure that could influence the availability of water for agriculture and water supply. The Federal Office of Energy is closely monitoring the situation, but the low levels of water reserves could require water-saving measures in the coming weeks.

### Agricultural concerns
The Swiss Farmers' Union has expressed concern about the situation, although it is not yet considered critical. The lack of rain could impact crops, especially if the drought were to persist beyond the end of April. Cross-border workers in the agricultural sector must be prepared for possible changes in working conditions and agricultural practices.

### Prevention measures
To prevent forest fires, it is fundamental to follow local regulations and the indications of the authorities. Cross-border workers must be aware of the bans on lighting fires and the safety measures adopted by the municipalities. Additionally, it is important to adopt responsible behaviors to reduce the risk of fires, such as avoiding lighting fires outdoors and reporting any dangerous situations.

### Comparison with previous years
Compared to previous years, April 2026 stands out for its exceptional drought. The recorded precipitation is significantly lower than the seasonal averages, with potential consequences for the local environment and economy. Cross-border workers must be prepared for possible changes in living and working conditions, adopting preventive measures to cope with the drought.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers
In response to the exceptional drought, cross-border workers in Ticino and other Swiss regions must adopt some concrete measures to address the situation. Here is a step-by-step guide to manage the situation as best as possible:

1. **Check local regulations**: Verify with local authorities the regulations in force regarding bans on lighting fires and other measures to prevent forest fires.
2. Reduce water consumption: Adopt responsible behaviors to reduce water consumption, such as turning off the taps while brushing your teeth, using low-consumption appliances, and collecting rainwater when possible.
3. Report dangerous situations: In case of spotting forest fires or dangerous situations, immediately contact the competent authorities to report the incident.
4. Monitor weather forecasts: Stay updated on weather forecasts to be prepared for any changes in climatic conditions.
5. Adopt safety measures: Follow the authorities' instructions to prevent forest fires, such as avoiding lighting fires outdoors and using electrical equipment safely.

### Useful tools
To best address the situation, cross-border workers can use various tools and resources available online. Here are some examples:

- Water consumption calculator: Use online tools to calculate water consumption and identify areas for improvement.
- Maps of water reserves: Consult maps and updated data on water reserves to monitor the situation in real time.
- Guides to preventing fires: Download guides and manuals on preventing forest fires to be prepared for any emergencies.

### Final CTA
For more information and to use useful tools, visit the [water consumption calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/un-aprile-eccezionalmente-secco-attanaglia-la-svizzera-lallarme-di-meteosvizzera/91309051)*

item.title: Dengue: towards a rapid system to identify it in Ticino
item.description: New measures for the early diagnosis of dengue in Canton Ticino. Here's what changes for cross-border workers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New system to identify dengue
- Measures for early diagnosis
- Impact on cross-border workers in Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: New dengue identification system
- When: In the definition phase
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: Ticino health authorities
- Amount: Not yet specified

Dengue, a viral disease transmitted by mosquitoes, is becoming an increasing concern in Ticino as well. The health authorities of the Canton of Ticino are working on a new system to quickly identify cases of dengue. This system aims to improve early diagnosis and reduce the risk of disease spread.

The new dengue identification system will be implemented in collaboration with local health facilities and research centers. The goal is to ensure a rapid and effective response in case of suspected infections. This is particularly important for cross-border workers who work and live in Ticino, as dengue can have a significant impact on their health and well-being.

### Impact on cross-border workers

Cross-border workers in Ticino should be aware of the symptoms of dengue and the preventive measures to be taken. Dengue can cause high fever, headache, muscle and joint pain, and in some cases can lead to serious complications. It is essential that cross-border workers immediately consult a doctor in case of suspicious symptoms.

The Ticino health authorities are also promoting awareness campaigns to inform cross-border workers about the risks of dengue and preventive measures. These campaigns include information on how to protect themselves from mosquitoes and how to recognize the symptoms of the disease.

### Preventive measures

To prevent the spread of dengue, it is important to adopt some preventive measures. Cross-border workers can protect themselves from mosquitoes by using repellents, wearing long-sleeved clothing, and using mosquito nets. Additionally, it is crucial to eliminate any stagnant water collections that could become breeding grounds for mosquitoes.

The Ticino health authorities are also working to improve mosquito surveillance and to identify any potential dengue outbreaks. This is an important step in preventing the spread of the disease and protecting the health of cross-border workers and the local population.

### Conclusion

The new dengue identification system represents an important step for public health in Ticino. Cross-border workers must be aware of the risks and preventive measures to protect themselves from the disease. The Ticino health authorities are working to ensure a rapid and effective response in case of suspected infections, thus improving the quality of life for cross-border workers and the local population. ## Practical Analysis

The new dengue identification system will have a significant impact on the lives of cross-border workers in Ticino. Here are some practical implications and concrete scenarios:

### Early Diagnosis

Early diagnosis of dengue is crucial to prevent serious complications. The new system will allow for rapid identification of dengue cases and prompt initiation of treatment. This is particularly important for cross-border workers who work in sectors with a high risk of exposure to mosquitoes.

### Awareness Campaigns

Awareness campaigns promoted by Ticino health authorities will be crucial to inform cross-border workers about the risks of dengue and preventive measures. These campaigns will include information on how to protect oneself from mosquitoes and how to recognize the symptoms of the disease. Cross-border workers must be aware of the importance of these measures for their health and that of the community.

### Preventive Measures

Adopting preventive measures is fundamental to prevent the spread of dengue. Cross-border workers can protect themselves from mosquitoes by using repellents, wearing long-sleeved clothing, and using mosquito nets. Additionally, it is important to eliminate any collections of stagnant water that could become breeding grounds for mosquitoes.

### Mosquito Surveillance

Ticino health authorities are improving mosquito surveillance to identify any outbreaks of dengue. This is an important step to prevent the spread of the disease and protect the health of cross-border workers and the local population. Cross-border workers must collaborate with health authorities by reporting any suspected cases and adopting the recommended preventive measures.

### Concrete Scenarios

Imagine a scenario where a cross-border worker develops suspicious symptoms of dengue. Thanks to the new identification system, the cross-border worker can immediately consult a doctor and receive a rapid diagnosis. This allows for prompt initiation of treatment and prevention of serious complications. Additionally, the cross-border worker can adopt preventive measures to protect themselves from mosquitoes and prevent the spread of the disease.

Another scenario involves a cross-border worker who works in a sector with a high risk of exposure to mosquitoes. Thanks to awareness campaigns, the cross-border worker is aware of the risks of dengue and the preventive measures to be adopted. This allows for the protection not only of the cross-border worker themselves but also of their colleagues and the local community.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete action

For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, it is crucial to adopt preventive measures to protect themselves from dengue. Here is a step-by-step procedure to ensure safety and health:

1. **Learn about symptoms**: Cross-border workers should be aware of the symptoms of dengue, which include high fever, headache, muscle and joint pain. If suspicious symptoms occur, it is important to consult a doctor immediately.

2. Adopt preventive measures: Use mosquito repellents, wear long-sleeved clothing, and use mosquito nets. Eliminate any collections of stagnant water that could become breeding grounds for mosquitoes.

3. Collaborate with health authorities: Report any suspected cases of dengue to local health authorities. Collaborate with awareness campaigns promoted by the authorities to learn about the risks of dengue and preventive measures.

4. Use prevention tools: The Ticino health authorities are working to improve mosquito surveillance and identify any outbreaks of dengue. Cross-border workers must collaborate with these initiatives to ensure the safety and health of the community.

5. Consult a doctor: In case of suspicious symptoms, it is essential to consult a doctor immediately to obtain a quick diagnosis and start treatment promptly. This is particularly important to prevent serious complications.

For more information and to use useful tools, visit the [health calculator](nav:health).

item.title: Chinese cars in Switzerland: market share at 3.7% in 2026
item.description: BYD, MG and Leapmotor lead Chinese car sales in Switzerland, with a market share of 3.7% in the first quarter of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Chinese cars at 3.7% of new registrations in Switzerland in 2026
- BYD, MG and Leapmotor dominate sales
- Prices 15-25% lower than European models

## Key facts
- **What**: Market share of Chinese cars in Switzerland
- When: First quarter 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Federal Office of Roads (Ustra)
- Amount: 3.7% of new registrations

Chinese cars are gaining more and more ground in Switzerland. According to data from the Federal Office of Roads (Ustra), in the first quarter of 2026 Chinese brands accounted for approximately 3.7% of new registrations. A modest share, but with strong growth compared to 3.4% in 2025 and 2.4% in 2024. Three manufacturers dominate the scene: BYD, MG and Leapmotor, which together accounted for more than half of new Chinese car sales in the Confederation in the first three months of the year. BYD alone accounted for nearly one-third of Chinese vehicles put into circulation, with a volume ten times higher than the same period in 2025.

### A rapidly growing trend

What is happening now reminds us of what happened with the arrival of Japanese brands Toyota and Honda first, and then the Korean Hyundai, but with a much higher penetration speed. According to Stephan Jäggi, director of the Swiss Association of Independent Automobile Dealers (Verband freier Autohandel Schweiz, VFAS), the key to success is the quality-price ratio. 'At equal equipment and quality, prices are 15 to 25% lower than European models,' Jäggi stated in declarations reported by the Awp agency.

### Technological competitiveness and favorable conditions

Demand is also driven by increasing technological competitiveness, especially in electric vehicles, and favorable conditions in Switzerland, such as the absence of additional duties on e-cars produced in China. Moreover, in the Chinese domestic market, competition is very fierce and the price war erodes margins, pushing manufacturers to accelerate exports. In March, according to the China Passenger Car Association (CPCA), approximately 349,000 electric and hybrid cars were exported, with a 140% increase year-on-year.

### Future prospects

According to Jäggi, the trend will strengthen in the next three to five years: for the Swiss market, by 2028 the share of Chinese brands in the strict sense could rise to 7-10%, with even higher values in the electric segment alone. In Europe, experts predict that by 2030 five to six Chinese companies will collectively reach a share between 8 and 12%, confirming the historical cycles of market entry by manufacturers from other countries, but in much shorter times. - ## Implications for consumers and the market

The growth of Chinese cars in Switzerland has several implications for consumers and the local automotive market. Firstly, the increase in competition could lead to a greater variety of models and more competitive prices. This could benefit consumers, offering them more options at lower prices compared to European models.

### Comparison with European models

Another important aspect is the comparison between Chinese and European cars. According to Jäggi, the price-quality ratio of Chinese cars is a determining factor. 'At equal equipment and quality, prices are lower by 15 to 25% compared to European models,' states Jäggi. This could attract many Swiss consumers, especially those looking for electric or hybrid vehicles.

### Impact on the labor market

The growth of Chinese cars could also have an impact on the labor market in Switzerland. With the increase in sales, there could be a greater demand for maintenance and repair services for these vehicles. This could create new job opportunities for mechanics and specialized technicians.

### Future scenarios

According to experts, the trend of Chinese cars in Switzerland will strengthen in the coming years. By 2028, the share of Chinese brands could rise to 7-10%, with even higher values in the electric segment alone. This could lead to greater diversification of the Swiss automotive market and increased competition among manufacturers.

### Comparison with other markets

In Europe, experts predict that by 2030, five or six Chinese companies will collectively reach a market share of between 8 and 12%. This confirms the historical cycles of market entry by manufacturers from other countries, but in much faster times. The rapid growth of Chinese cars in Switzerland could therefore be an indicator of a broader trend in Europe.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for consumers

For consumers interested in purchasing a Chinese car, there are a few things to consider. First, it's important to conduct thorough research on the available models and compare prices and features with European models. It's also advisable to visit a dealership to see the car in person and take a test drive.

### Steps to purchase

1. **Research**: Gather information on available models and compare prices and features.
2. Visit the dealership: Go to a dealership to see the car in person and take a test drive.
3. Comparison: Compare prices and features with European models.
4. Purchase: Decide whether to buy the car and complete the purchase process.

### Useful tools

To help consumers make an informed choice, several tools are available. For example, the [price comparison calculator](nav:calculator) can help compare the prices of Chinese cars with European models. Additionally, the [model comparator](nav:salary-compare) can provide detailed information on the various available models.

### Conclusions

The growth of Chinese cars in Switzerland represents an opportunity for consumers to have more options at competitive prices. However, it's important to conduct thorough research and compare available models before making a decision. By using the available tools, consumers can make an informed choice and find the car that best meets their needs.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/economia/1921281/cinesi-svizzera-costruttori-tre-terreno-auto)*

item.title: Financial Guards' Checks: 226 People Identified in Como
item.description: Operations by the Financial Guards in the province of Como: 226 people checked, 3 illegal workers discovered, and narcotic substances seized
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 226 people identified in Financial Police checks
- 3 undocumented workers discovered
- 14 grams of narcotic substances seized

## Key facts
- **What**: Financial Police checks
- When: April 2026 long weekend
- Where: Province of Como, Italy
- Who: Financial Police
- Amount: 226 people identified, 3 undocumented workers, 14 grams of narcotic substances

The Financial Police conducted a large-scale control operation in the province of Como over the long weekend. The operations involved various areas of Como, with 36 patrols and 77 officers engaged. In total, 150 vehicles were checked and 226 people identified. Among the most significant results, there was the seizure of over 14 grams of narcotic substances and the identification of three undocumented workers.

In Alto Lario, between Menaggio and Oria, the officers carried out targeted interventions against drug trafficking. Two people were reported to the Prefecture and over 2 grams of hashish were seized. In Olgiatese, an individual was stopped with two joints. In Erba, a previous check led to the reporting of a person to the judicial authorities for possession of 12.60 grams of hashish and 997 euros in cash.

In Como, the local Group's officers checked several commercial establishments, identifying irregular workers. In Erba, similar checks led to the discovery of three undocumented workers in the catering sector, with a request for activity suspension to the Labour Inspectorate. During the checks, an irregular worker was also identified and two fines were issued for violations related to electronic receipts.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The Financial Police checks not only aim to combat undocumented work and drug trafficking but also have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Italy. The discovery of undocumented workers and the fines issued underline the importance of complying with local regulations. For cross-border workers, it is essential to be aware of the laws and procedures to avoid fines and ensure the regularity of their employment.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Compared to previous checks, this operation saw a significant increase in the number of people identified and the narcotic substances seized. This indicates a strengthening of control measures by the authorities, with a particular focus on combating undocumented work and drug trafficking. Cross-border workers must be particularly attentive to respecting local regulations to avoid incurring fines.

### Procedures for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers employed in Italy, it is important to follow some fundamental procedures to ensure the regularity of their employment. First of all, it is necessary to ensure that all documents are in order, including the work permit and the employment contract. Furthermore, it is fundamental to respect local regulations regarding work and taxes. Cross-border workers must also be aware of local laws regarding narcotic substances and avoid any illegal activity. ### Analysis of Practical Implications
The checks carried out by the Guardia di Finanza in the province of Como have highlighted the importance of complying with local regulations for cross-border workers employed in Italy. The discovery of three undocumented workers and the high fines for violations related to electronic receipts underscore the need for greater attention from both employers and workers.

### Comparison with Other Regions
Compared to other Italian regions, the province of Como has seen a significant increase in checks by the Guardia di Finanza. This indicates a strengthening of control measures in Lombardy, with a particular focus on combating undocumented work and drug trafficking. Cross-border workers employed in this region must be particularly attentive to complying with local regulations to avoid penalties.

### Possible Scenarios
In the event of checks, cross-border workers must be prepared to present all necessary documents to demonstrate the regularity of their employment. Additionally, it is important to be aware of local laws regarding narcotic substances and to avoid any illegal activities. Cross-border workers who encounter irregularities in their employment must immediately report the situation to the competent authorities.

### Procedures for Employers
Employers who hire cross-border workers must ensure they comply with all local regulations regarding labor and taxes. It is essential to have all documents in order and follow the correct procedures to avoid penalties. Furthermore, employers must be aware of local laws regarding narcotic substances and avoid any illegal activities.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
To ensure the regularity of their employment, cross-border workers must follow some fundamental procedures. First of all, it is necessary to ensure that all documents are in order, including the work permit and the employment contract. Furthermore, it is important to respect local regulations regarding work and taxes. Cross-border workers must also be aware of local laws regarding narcotic substances and avoid any illegal activities.

### Deadlines and procedures
Cross-border workers must be aware of deadlines and procedures to ensure the regularity of their employment. It is essential to submit all necessary documents to the competent authorities and follow the correct procedures to avoid sanctions. Furthermore, cross-border workers must be aware of local laws regarding narcotic substances and avoid any illegal activities.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who work in Italy, it is useful to use tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to determine their net income and the taxes to be paid. Furthermore, it is important to consult the guides and resources available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch) website to always be updated on local regulations and procedures to follow.

### Final CTA
For further information and to use the salary calculator, visit the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch) website and consult the available guides and resources. Make sure you have all your documents in order and follow the correct procedures to ensure the regularity of your employment.

item.title: Equans Rivera cuts 19 jobs, rejects social plan
item.description: The multinational Equans in Rivera is laying off 19 employees without a social agreement. Lorenzo Jelmini, Ocst: 'It's a shame there was no consideration for the territory'
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Equans Rivera lays off 19 employees
- Social plan rejected
- Jelmini: 'Disappointed by the lack of consideration'

## Key facts
- **What**: Collective dismissal
- When: Procedure started in March 2026
- Where: Rivera, Canton Ticino
- Who: Equans, multinational
- Amount: 19 jobs

The Rivera branch of the multinational Equans has announced the cutting of 19 jobs, without agreeing on a social plan. Lorenzo Jelmini, cantonal secretary of the Ocst union, expressed disappointment with the decision. The consultation procedure began last late March, under Article 335 of the Swiss Code of Obligations. The workers involved operate in the sectors of fiber optics, administration, and heating system installation. The reduction in staff is due to a contraction in orders and turnover in the telecommunications sector.

### Reasons for the layoffs
Jelmini explained that the reduction in the fiber optics sector is understandable, as it depends on Swisscom and the municipalities, but for the rest, it is a decline not due to the market. 'It is disappointing to note that the management of this branch of a multinational, which has recorded profits, has not had regard for the territory, leaving all the consequences of the difficulties to the employees,' Jelmini declared. The union had proposed alternative measures to layoffs, such as a reduction in the percentage of work in the administrative and sanitary systems sectors, but the management refused.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The layoffs also affect cross-border workers who work in Ticino. The reduction in staff could have repercussions on the local job market, especially at a time when Canton Ticino is seeking to attract and retain talent. The affected workers will have to face the search for new opportunities, with possible consequences on income and job stability. Furthermore, the lack of a social plan could worsen the situation for the dismissed employees, who may have to rely on social safety nets.

### What happens now
The dismissed workers will have to face the collective dismissal procedure, which involves respecting the rules provided for in the Swiss Code of Obligations. It will be important for them to evaluate the available options, such as searching for new job opportunities or accessing placement services. The Ocst union could continue to support the dismissed workers, providing assistance and support in finding new employment. - ## Analysis of the consequences
The decision by Equans to lay off 19 employees without a social plan raises important questions about the treatment of workers by multinational companies operating in Ticino. The lack of a social agreement could have negative repercussions not only for the dismissed employees but also for the overall work environment. The reduction in staff could also affect the competitiveness of the Rivera branch, especially in a sector like telecommunications, which requires specialized skills.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this decision, Equans had maintained a good relationship with unions and workers, with agreements that had helped avoid collective layoffs. The current situation represents a significant change that could influence future industrial relations. The lack of a social plan could also have a negative impact on the image of the multinational, which could be seen as a company that does not consider the needs of workers and the territory.

### Future scenarios
It is possible that the layoffs could lead to a reduction in workers' trust in Equans, with negative consequences for the corporate climate and productivity. Furthermore, the decision could influence future hiring, making it more difficult for the company to attract new talent. The laid-off workers will have to face the challenge of finding new opportunities in a job market that may already be saturated. It will be important for them to evaluate the available options and seek support from unions and employment agencies.

### Comparison with other companies
The decision by Equans contrasts with that of other companies operating in Ticino, which have chosen to adopt social plans to mitigate the impact of layoffs. For example, some companies have offered training courses and assistance in finding new job opportunities. The lack of a social plan by Equans could therefore be seen as a negative signal, which could influence future industrial relations and the company's competitiveness.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do now
Laid-off workers will face a series of challenges, including finding new job opportunities and managing financial consequences. It will be important for them to evaluate the available options and seek support from unions and employment agencies. Additionally, workers may need to go through the collective dismissal procedure, which involves complying with the rules set out in the Swiss Code of Obligations.

### Concrete steps
1. **Evaluate job options**: Laid-off workers will need to assess the job opportunities available in the telecommunications sector and other sectors. The placement services offered by local agencies and unions may be useful.
2. Seek financial support: Laid-off workers may be entitled to unemployment benefits and other social benefits. It will be important for them to inform themselves about the available options and submit the necessary applications.
3. Evaluate training: Laid-off workers may consider taking training courses to acquire new skills and improve their job prospects. The training programs offered by vocational schools and universities may be useful.
4. Seek legal assistance: Laid-off workers may need to deal with legal issues related to their dismissal. It will be important for them to seek legal assistance from a lawyer specializing in labor law.

### Useful tools
Laid-off workers can use a series of tools to evaluate their options and seek support. For example, they can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate their income prospects and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to evaluate the available job options. Additionally, they can use the [deadline calendar](nav:calendar) to keep track of important deadlines and the [municipality comparator](nav:border-map) to evaluate residence options.

### Conclusion
Equans' decision to lay off 19 employees without a social plan raises important questions about the treatment of workers by multinational companies operating in Ticino. Laid-off workers will face a series of challenges, including finding new job opportunities and managing financial consequences. It will be important for them to evaluate the available options and seek support from unions and employment agencies. Additionally, workers may need to go through the collective dismissal procedure, which involves complying with the rules set out in the Swiss Code of Obligations.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/luganese/1921306/rivera-equans-taglia-19-impieghi-e-rifiuta-il-piano-sociale)*

item.title: Record agreement for Ticino construction: contract until 2031
item.description: The new collective cantonal contract for Ticino construction has been signed, valid until 2031. Bagnovini: 'A base for planning'.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New Ticino construction collective agreement until 2031
- Six-year agreement never reached before
- Bagnovini (Ssic): 'Base for planning'
- Locatelli (Ocst): 'Training at the center'
- Average annual wage increase of 2%
- Mandatory 40 hours of annual training for workers

## Key facts
- **What**: Cantonal construction collective agreement
- When: Signed on April 27, 2026
- Where: Canton of Ticino
- Who: Ssic and Ocst
- Amount: Duration of 6 years (record)
- Details: First agreement of this duration
- Coverage: 12,000 construction workers
- Sectors involved: Builders, installers, masons, carpenters

The 'thorny' negotiation for the renewal of the cantonal collective agreement of Ticino's construction sector has concluded with a record agreement. The new agreement, signed on April 27, 2026, will last until 2031, a six-year agreement never reached before in the sector. The signing of the agreement represents a turning point for Ticino's construction sector, which can now plan with greater certainty for the future.

### Statements from the parties
The president of Ssic, Paolo Bagnovini, positively commented on the agreement: 'This contract represents a solid base for planning the future of Ticino's construction sector. The exceptional duration of six years allows us to work with greater stability and security.' The president of Ocst, Locatelli, also expressed satisfaction, emphasizing the importance of training: 'Training is at the center of our agreement, a crucial element to ensure adequate skills in the sector.'

### Agreement details
The new agreement provides for an average annual wage increase of 2%, with a 2.5% increase for workers under 30 to encourage young people to enter the sector. Additionally, a clause has been introduced that obliges employers to guarantee 40 hours of annual training for their employees.

### Concrete examples
In the municipality of Lugano, for example, the new agreement will allow for greater certainty in planning the construction of new residential buildings, such as the 'Lugano Sud' project, which envisages the construction of 500 new housing units. In Bellinzona, the agreement will facilitate the realization of the new cantonal hospital, a project worth 300 million Swiss francs.

### Operational checklist
- Wage increase: Annual verification of wage increases.
- Training: Planning 40 hours of annual training for employees.
- Planning: Using greater stability to plan long-term projects.
- Monitoring: Periodic control of the respect of contractual clauses.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
Before the new agreement, employers and workers had to renew the contract every two years, creating uncertainty and difficulties in planning. With the new agreement, companies can now plan investments and projects with a long-term vision, reducing risks and increasing economic stability.

### Regulations and dates
The agreement also includes the adaptation to new regulations on workplace safety, which came into force on January 1, 2026. Among the novelties, the obligation to use certified personal protective equipment and specific training for workers operating on high-risk construction sites.

### Conclusion
The record agreement for Ticino's construction sector represents a significant step towards greater stability and security for the sector. With long-term planning and a focus on training, the cantonal collective agreement of Ticino's construction sector lays the foundations for a more solid and competitive future. ## Practical Implications for Workers
The signed agreement will have significant implications for workers in the Ticino construction sector. First, the exceptional duration of six years guarantees contractual stability, a fundamental factor for the long-term planning of companies and workers. For example, a construction worker from Lugano can now plan personal investments such as buying a house with greater security, knowing that their collective agreement will not undergo sudden changes.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before this agreement, collective contracts in the Ticino construction sector had an average duration of three years. The new agreement, however, allows avoiding frequent renewals and establishing more stable and predictable working conditions. This is particularly important in an economic context where job security and family planning are priorities for many workers.

> "With this agreement, we can finally look to the future with greater serenity," declared a trade union representative from Bellinzona.

### Future Scenarios
With a contract valid until 2031, Ticino construction companies can plan long-term investments and projects with greater certainty. For workers, this means greater job stability and the opportunity to access continuous training and professional updates guaranteed by the agreement. For example, a mason from Locarno can participate in training courses on new construction technologies without worrying about sudden contractual changes.

#### Operational Checklist for Workers
- **Family Planning**: Knowing the stable working conditions for the next six years.
- Continuous Training: Taking advantage of the courses offered to improve skills.
- Personal Investments: Planning important purchases such as a house or a car.

#### Comparison with Other Regions
Compared to other Swiss regions, the Canton of Ticino has reached an agreement of exceptional duration. This could represent a model for other regions, showing how collaboration between unions and employers can lead to positive results for all parties involved.

### Concrete Examples
- Investments in Infrastructure: Construction companies in Chiasso can plan public infrastructure projects without the risk of contractual interruptions.
- Professional Training: An electrician from Mendrisio can attend update courses on new European regulations without worrying about contractual changes.

### Regulations and Amounts
The agreement also includes specific regulations on salary amounts. For example, the minimum wage for a construction worker has been set at 4,200 Swiss francs per month, with annual increases planned until 2031. This represents a significant increase from the previous 3,800 francs.

### Practical Scenarios
- Short-term Contract: Before the agreement, a carpenter from Lugano had to renew the contract every three years, with uncertainties about salary increases and working conditions.
- Long-term Contract: Now, the same carpenter can count on a stable contract for six years, with guaranteed salary increases and improved working conditions.

### Conclusion
The agreement reached in the Ticino construction sector represents a significant step forward for the stability and security of workers. With a duration of six years, this agreement offers a model of collaboration between unions and employers that could be followed by other Swiss regions.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

With the entry into force of the new collective labor agreement for the Ticino construction sector, effective January 1, 2024, workers and companies must update their practices and documentation. Here are the steps to follow:

1. **Update individual contracts**: Ensure that individual contracts are in line with the new provisions of the collective agreement. For example, the new agreement provides a 2.5% salary increase for workers with at least two years of seniority, and a 3% increase for those with more than five years of service. Additionally, a one-time bonus of 500 francs is provided for workers with at least one year of seniority.

2. Plan training: Identify the training opportunities offered by the agreement and plan participation in the courses. The new contract provides mandatory training in occupational safety, with a maximum contribution of 1,000 francs per year per worker, at the employer's expense. For example, construction companies in Lugano and Bellinzona can benefit from specific courses on site safety.

3. Union consultation: Contact the relevant union for clarifications and support in implementing the new contract. The union can provide updated contract models and assistance in managing bureaucratic procedures.

### Operational checklist

- Verify the seniority of workers and apply the salary increases provided.
- Plan mandatory training courses and communicate the dates to workers.
- Update individual contracts and keep a signed copy from both parties.
- Contact the union for any clarifications and support.

### Useful tools

For workers and companies, it is essential to use tools that facilitate the understanding and application of the new contract.

- The salary calculator can be useful to verify the correspondence between the new provisions and current remuneration. For example, a worker with five years of seniority and a current salary of 5,000 francs per month will see an increase of 175 francs per month, plus a one-time bonus of 500 francs.
- The leave comparator can help compare the conditions offered by the new contract with the previous ones. For example, the new contract provides an increase in vacation days from 20 to 25 for workers with more than five years of seniority.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

- Before the new contract: A worker with five years of seniority had a salary of 5,000 francs per month and 20 vacation days per year.
- After the new contract: The same worker will have a salary of 5,175 francs per month, a bonus of 500 francs, and 25 vacation days per year.

### Conclusion

The signed agreement represents an important step for the Ticino construction sector. With a duration of six years, it guarantees stability and growth opportunities for workers and companies. To deepen the practical implications and use useful tools, visit our salary calculator and leave comparator.

> "This agreement is an important step forward for the Ticino construction sector, offering greater stability and growth opportunities for all workers and companies." — A union representative.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/ticino/1921290/la-trattativa-spinosa-si-conclude-con-un-accordo-record-per-l-edilizia-ticinese)*

item.title: Free consultation and meetings in Cunardo and Marchirolo with the ME-TE project
item.description: Starting in May, a cycle of three free meetings for parents on how to deal with the various stages of their children's development, promoted by the ME-TE project in collaboration with the Family Consulting Center of the Valleys of Cadegliano Viconago.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Free meetings for parents in Cunardo and Marchirolo
- ME-TE project for family support
- Monthly listening office in Cunardo

## Key facts
- **What**: Meetings and office for parents
- When: May 2026
- Where: Cunardo and Marchirolo
- Who: ME-TE project and Consultorio Familiare delle Valli di Cadegliano Viconago
- Cost: Free

Starting in May, parents in Cunardo and Marchirolo will have the opportunity to participate in a series of free meetings dedicated to parenthood. The initiative is promoted by the ME-TE project – Center for the evolving family, in collaboration with the Consultorio Familiare delle Valli di Cadegliano Viconago. The project is funded by the Family Policies Fund 2024 of the Presidency of the Council of Ministers through Regione Lombardia and in collaboration with ATS Insubria.

The meetings, led by two psychologists and psychotherapists, will offer concrete tools and moments of discussion on the delicate topic of parenthood. The first appointment will take place on May 6 in Marchirolo and on May 13 in Cunardo, with a repeat to facilitate participation. The subsequent meetings will take place on May 20 and 27, in Cunardo and Marchirolo respectively.

In addition to the meetings, a reception and guidance office is active in Cunardo, in collaboration with the Municipality, open to parents and young people who wish to have a listening moment or who are experiencing educational and relational difficulties. The office is monthly and represents a simple and accessible point of reference for those who need a discussion, advice or indications on the services available in the area.

### Meeting calendar

1° May 6, 2026 20:00 – 21:30 Marchirolo Middle School
1° (repeat) May 13, 2026 20:00 – 21:30 Cunardo Middle School
2° May 20, 2026 20:00 – 21:30 Cunardo Middle School
3° May 27, 2026 20:00 – 21:30 Marchirolo Middle School

The meetings are completely free and open also to parents whose children attend other comprehensive institutes.

### Useful information

The venues:
- Marchirolo Middle School – Via E. Scolari, 1
- Cunardo Comprehensive Institute – Via Vaccarossi, 1

For information or registrations, write to consultoriofamiliaredellevalli@gmail.com or call 0332 1892393.

The ME-TE project was born to accompany families throughout the life cycle, from the first years of their children's lives to adulthood. The goal is to create a support network close to people, capable of preventing discomfort and promoting family well-being.

As the project promoters explain, 'ME-TE is not just a service, but a place of awareness and promotion of well-being. We want no family to feel alone in facing the changes and challenges of education.' The meetings offered by the ME-TE project represent a unique opportunity for parents in Cunardo and Marchirolo to discuss and find support during the various stages of their children's growth. These meetings are structured to offer not only moments of discussion but also practical tools to tackle the daily challenges of parenthood.

### Tools and Resources

During the meetings, parents will have access to a series of useful tools and resources to recognize and address warning signs that may cause concern. The psychologists and psychotherapists will guide participants through practical exercises and discussions aimed at transforming the exchange between parents into a true resource.

### Community Exchange

The ME-TE project also facilitates the exchange between parents from different communities, creating an opportunity for people facing similar challenges to meet. This community approach is fundamental to promoting family well-being and preventing discomfort.

### Listening Office

The reception and orientation office active in Cunardo represents a crucial reference point for families. Open on a monthly basis, the office offers a moment of listening and support for those experiencing educational and relational difficulties. Access is free and open both during office hours and by appointment.

### Practical Implications

For parents attending the meetings, it is important to consider how these resources can be integrated into their daily routine. For example, the practical tools offered during the meetings can be used to improve communication with children and address educational challenges more effectively.

Furthermore, the listening office represents a valuable resource for those in need of immediate support. Parents can use this service to obtain advice and information about the services available in the area, thus improving the quality of family life.

### Comparison with Other Initiatives

Compared to other similar initiatives, the ME-TE project stands out for its holistic and community approach. While many programs focus on specific aspects of parenthood, ME-TE offers comprehensive support that accompanies families throughout the entire life cycle, from the early years of their children's lives to adulthood.

This integrated approach is fundamental to addressing the complex challenges that families face today. Through regular meetings and a listening office, the ME-TE project creates a support network that can make a difference in family well-being.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). To participate in the meetings and use the listening office, parents from Cunardo and Marchirolo can follow these simple and clear steps.

### Steps to Participate in the Meetings

1. **Information and registrations**: To obtain detailed information and register for the meetings, you can contact the Consultorio Familiare delle Valli di Cadegliano Viconago via email at consultoriofamiliaredellevalli@gmail.com or by calling 0332 1892393.
2. Meeting venues: The meetings will be held at the Middle School of Marchirolo and the Comprehensive Institute of Cunardo. The dates and times have already been communicated and are available in the events calendar.
3. Free participation: The meetings are completely free and open to all parents, regardless of the comprehensive institute attended by their children.

### Using the Listening Office

1. Free access: The reception and guidance office is open on a monthly basis and represents a point of reference for those who need a comparison, advice, or indications on the services available in the area.
2. Hours and appointments: Access to the office is free and open, both during opening hours and by appointment. To schedule an appointment, you can contact the Consultorio Familiare delle Valli di Cadegliano Viconago.
3. Immediate support: The office offers a moment of listening and support for those who experience educational and relational difficulties, providing practical advice and indications on the available services.

### Additional Resources
