In addition to the meetings and the listening office, parents can find further resources and support through the services offered by the Consultorio Familiare delle Valli di Cadegliano Viconago. These services include individual consultations, support groups, and educational activities for children and adolescents.

### Conclusion

The ME-TE project represents a valuable opportunity for parents in Cunardo and Marchirolo to find support and resources to face the challenges of parenthood. Through free meetings and a listening office, the project offers comprehensive support that can make a difference in family well-being.

For more information and to participate in the meetings, contact the Consultorio Familiare delle Valli di Cadegliano Viconago.

[Learn more about the ME-TE project](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/sportello-e-incontri-gratuiti-a-cunardo-e-marchirolo-con-il-progetto-me-te/2559241/)*

item.title: Bracco Scholarships: 11 opportunities for students from six Lombard municipalities
item.description: The Bracco Foundation has opened the call for 11 scholarships, with contributions up to 4,000 euros, for deserving students from Bovisio Masciago, Ceriano Laghetto, Cesano Maderno, Cogliate, Limbiate, and Solaro.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Open call for 11 Bracco Foundation scholarships
- Contributions up to 4,000 euros for deserving students
- Residency required in six Lombard communes

## Key facts
- **What**: Scholarship call
- When: Applications by October 15, 2026
- Where: Communes of Bovisio Masciago, Ceriano Laghetto, Cesano Maderno, Cogliate, Limbiate, Solaro
- Who: Bracco Foundation, Bracco Imaging Spa, municipal administrations
- Amount: 6 scholarships of 3,000 euros, 1 scholarship of 3,000 euros in memory of Emilio Giudici, 4 scholarships of 4,000 euros

The call for scholarships from the Bracco Foundation is back, an initiative involving six Lombard communes aimed at supporting the university path of deserving students. The new edition is open to young residents of Bovisio Masciago, Ceriano Laghetto, Cesano Maderno, Cogliate, Limbiate, and Solaro. The competition, promoted by the Bracco Foundation in collaboration with Bracco Imaging Spa and the involved municipal administrations, offers a total of 11 scholarships.

In detail, the following are provided: 6 scholarships of 3,000 euros each reserved for students in their first three years of university, both in humanities and scientific disciplines; 1 scholarship of 3,000 euros dedicated to first-year students, established in memory of Emilio Giudici, mayor of Ceriano Laghetto from 1980 to 1990; 4 scholarships of 4,000 euros each destined for students in their second two years.

The initiative is part of the "I Will Become" project by the Bracco Foundation, which for years has accompanied students in their educational path, valuing merit and promoting the link between territory, education, and the world of work. The call aims to concretely support young talents and facilitate access to university studies, rewarding commitment and academic results.

Applications must be submitted by October 15, 2026, through the online platform. Among the required criteria are residency in one of the involved communes, enrollment in a degree course, and an Isee value within the limits set by the call.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing in one of the involved communes, this initiative represents a concrete opportunity to support the university education costs of their children. The scholarships can help cover tuition fees, teaching materials, and other needs related to the academic path. Additionally, the call rewards merit, encouraging students to strive for excellent results.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Compared to previous editions, the 2026 call by the Bracco Foundation offers an increase in the number of available scholarships, with a higher maximum contribution. This represents a significant step in supporting higher education, especially in an economic context that can be challenging for many families.

### Deadlines and procedures

Applications must be submitted by October 15, 2026, through the online platform. It is important to verify the specific requirements of the call and prepare all the necessary documentation, including the Isee certification and enrollment in a degree course. Interested students should start gathering the required documents as soon as possible to avoid delays.

### Useful tools

For further information and to submit the application, you can visit the official website of the Bracco Foundation. Additionally, cross-border workers can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to better plan the expenses related to university education. ## Practical Analysis: How to Benefit from the Grant

The grant for the scholarships of the Bracco Foundation represents an important opportunity for deserving students residing in the six Lombard municipalities involved. To maximize the chances of success, it is essential to carefully follow the instructions of the grant and submit a complete and accurate application.

### Steps to Submit the Application

1. **Check the requirements**: Make sure you reside in one of the indicated municipalities and are enrolled in a degree course. Also check the income limits provided by the grant.
2. Gather documentation: Prepare all the necessary documents, including the Isee certification, the university enrollment certificate, and any other required document.
3. Fill out the application: Use the online platform to fill out the participation application. Make sure to provide all the requested information in a precise and complete manner.
4. Submit the application: Submit the application by October 15, 2026. Keep a copy of the application and the submission receipt for any future checks.

### Tips for a Successful Application

- Be precise and detailed: Provide all the requested information in a clear and accurate manner. Avoid errors or omissions that could compromise your application.
- Highlight your academic achievements: Emphasize your school and university successes, demonstrating your commitment and dedication to your studies.
- Respect deadlines: Make sure to submit the application by the established deadline to avoid being excluded from the grant.

### Comparison with Other Funding Opportunities

In addition to the scholarships of the Bracco Foundation, there are other funding opportunities for university students. For example, many universities offer internal scholarships based on merit or income. Furthermore, it is possible to request honor loans or contributions from local authorities or associations.

### Tax Implications

For cross-border workers residing in Lombardy and working in Switzerland, it is important to consider the tax implications of the scholarships. Scholarships are generally tax-exempt, but it is always advisable to consult a tax expert to avoid surprises.

### Useful Tools

To better plan the expenses related to university education, cross-border workers can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) and the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator). Additionally, you can consult the tax guides available on the site to better understand the tax implications of the scholarships.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for interested students

For students interested in applying for the Bracco Foundation scholarships, it is crucial to act promptly and precisely. Here is a step-by-step guide to submitting the application and maximizing your chances of success.

### Step 1: Verify the requirements

Before starting the application, it is essential to verify that you meet all the requirements specified in the call. In particular, you must:
- Reside in one of the indicated municipalities (Bovisio Masciago, Ceriano Laghetto, Cesano Maderno, Cogliate, Limbiate, Solaro).
- Be enrolled in a degree course.
- Have an Isee value within the limits set by the call.

### Step 2: Gather the documentation

Once the requirements have been verified, it is important to gather all the necessary documentation. The required documents include:
- Isee certification.
- University enrollment certificate.
- Valid ID document.
- Any other documents specified in the call.

### Step 3: Fill out the application

The application must be completed through the online platform indicated in the call. It is fundamental to provide all the requested information accurately and completely. Make sure to:
- Fill in all mandatory fields.
- Attach all required documents.
- Verify the accuracy of the information provided.

### Step 4: Submit the application

Once the application has been completed, it must be submitted by October 15, 2026. Keep a copy of the application and the submission receipt for any future checks.

### Step 5: Monitor communications

After submitting the application, it is important to monitor the official communications from the Bracco Foundation. Additional information or documents may be requested. Make sure to respond promptly to any requests to avoid delays in the evaluation of your application.

### Useful tools

To better plan the expenses related to university education, cross-border workers can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) and the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator). Additionally, you can consult the tax guides available on the site to better understand the tax implications of scholarships.

### Conclusion

The call for the Bracco Foundation scholarships represents a concrete opportunity to support the university path of deserving students. By carefully following the call's instructions and submitting a complete and accurate application, you can maximize your chances of success. Do not miss this opportunity to invest in your academic and professional future.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/aperto-il-bando-per-le-borse-di-studio-della-fondazione-bracco-per-i-giovani-di-sei-comuni-lombardi/2559106/)*

item.title: Green Ticino: strategy for the 2026 cantonal elections
item.description: The Green Party of Ticino is exploring alliances for the 2026 cantonal elections, aiming for a broader progressive front.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Greens Ticino explore alliances for 2026 cantonal elections
- Goal: progressive front with MPS and PS
- Greta Gysin might run for the State Council

## Key facts
- **What**: Strategy for the 2026 cantonal elections
- When: Assembly held on April 26, 2026
- Where: Locarno, Ticino
- Who: Greens of Ticino
- Amount: Not yet specified

The Greens of Ticino are working to define their strategy ahead of the 2026 cantonal elections. During an assembly held in Locarno on April 26, 2026, the party tasked its coordination with exploring possible alliances to present a broader progressive front. 'We have been given the mandate to explore the alliance certainly with the MPS and the PS, with whom we are already in contact,' explained Samantha Bourgoin, co-coordinator of the party, to the microphones of SEIDISERA. Moreover, the Greens intend to deepen contacts with other formations, such as Più Donne and the PC.

Analyses following the 2023 elections had highlighted that part of the Greens' electorate identifies with the party's ecological policies but not with its social policies. Furthermore, the alliance with the PS had resulted in a performance below expectations. However, Bourgoin emphasized that the Greens are convinced that their work in Parliament can make a difference. 'Because as Greens, we are certainly the most credible and the most aggressive in advancing our themes,' she stated.

Regarding government action, Bourgoin deemed 'dangerous' a right that gains ground by blaming the left and the Greens for the deficits. 'Together with other forces, with other movements, we would be stronger,' she added. For the State Council, many await a 'yes' for the Government race from the current national councilor Greta Gysin. However, it is still too early to think about names. The objectives, however, are clear: if on the right UDC and Lega do not run together, interesting paths open up.

### Goals and Alliances

The Greens of Ticino aim to create a progressive front that can effectively counter the right. The alliance with MPS and PS is considered fundamental, but collaborations with other political forces are not excluded. This strategic approach could allow the Greens to achieve better results compared to previous elections, where the alliance with the PS had not met expectations.

Co-coordinator Samantha Bourgoin emphasized the importance of presenting themselves as a credible and aggressive party on ecological issues. This could attract a broader electorate, even among those who do not identify with the party's social policies. Furthermore, the alliance with other political forces could help counter the narrative of the right, which often blames the left and the Greens for the deficits.

### Possible Candidacies

Among the names that could emerge for the State Council race is that of Greta Gysin, current national councilor. However, it is still too early to confirm any candidacy. The Greens of Ticino are working to define a clear and coherent strategy that can lead to positive results in the 2026 elections. The goal is to present themselves as a united and determined front, capable of facing future challenges successfully. ## Analysis of Implications

The strategy of the Ticino Greens to explore alliances with other progressive political forces could have a significant impact on the 2026 cantonal elections. This approach could attract a broader electorate, including those sensitive to ecological issues but not necessarily to the social policies promoted by the Greens. Furthermore, a united progressive front could more effectively counter the narrative of the right, which often blames economic deficits on the left and the Greens.

### Comparison with Previous Elections

In the 2023 elections, the alliance between the Greens and the PS did not meet expectations, partly due to the divergence between ecological and social policies. This time, the Greens intend to present a broader front that can also include other political groups. This could help overcome internal divisions and present a more united and coherent image to the electorate.

### Possible Scenarios

If the alliance with MPS and PS materializes, the Greens could achieve better results compared to previous elections. Additionally, the possibility of collaborating with other political forces, such as Più Donne and the PC, could further expand the electoral base. However, it is important that the party maintains its credibility on ecological issues, which represent one of its strengths.

### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino, the 2026 cantonal elections could have a significant impact on local policies, particularly those related to the environment and sustainability. A united progressive front could promote more ambitious policies in these sectors, with possible benefits for the quality of life and the environment. Furthermore, collaboration between different political forces could lead to greater stability and cohesion in the cantonal government.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for citizens

For Ticino citizens and cross-border workers interested in actively participating in the electoral process, it's important to closely follow the developments of political alliances and official candidacies. Here are some concrete steps that can be taken:

1. **Stay informed about alliances**: Follow news and official statements from the Ticino Greens and other political forces involved to better understand the dynamics of the alliances.
2. Attend local events: Participate in public meetings and debates organized by the parties to express your opinions and get to know the candidates better.
3. Register to vote: Make sure you are registered to vote and know the dates and locations of the elections.
4. Use comparison tools: Use online tools like the [election calculator](nav:calculator) to compare the proposals of different parties and candidates.
5. Participate in awareness initiatives: Join campaigns and initiatives promoted by the Greens and other political forces to promote ecological and social issues.

### Useful tools

To delve deeper into the 2026 cantonal elections and the policies of the Ticino Greens, you can consult the party's official website and use tools like the [election calculator](nav:calculator) to compare the proposals of different parties. Additionally, it's useful to follow the news and official statements to stay updated on the developments of alliances and candidacies.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3694293)*

item.title: Equans lays off 19 employees in Monteceneri: the details
item.description: Equans has announced 19 layoffs at its Monteceneri site, affecting the optical fiber and facility management sectors.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 19 employees laid off by Equans in Monteceneri
- Sectors involved: fiber optics and facility management
- Decline in turnover for the Ticino branch

## Key facts
- **What**: Layoffs
- When: Recently announced
- Where: Monteceneri, Ticino
- Who: Equans
- Amount: 19 employees

Nineteen employees of Equans, a branch based in Monteceneri of the group formed in 2018 from the merger with Alpiq, have been laid off. The decision, anticipated by the Corriere del Ticino, was announced to the OCST union by the company's management in the past few hours. The affected employees, as always reported by the Corriere del Ticino, are free to leave immediately and can leave the company before the notice period if they find another job.

The number of jobs cut is slightly lower than that announced in March, when 20-22 positions were mentioned. The sectors affected by the layoffs are three, particularly fiber optics, heating system management, and the administrative sector related to these two activities.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The layoffs at Equans in Monteceneri could have significant repercussions for cross-border workers employed in Ticino. With the decline in turnover of the Ticino branch, where around 220 people currently work, the situation could become more complex for those seeking job opportunities in the region. Cross-border workers who depend on sectors such as heating system management and fiber optics may have to face greater uncertainty in the job market.

Furthermore, cross-border workers employed by Equans may have to consider the tax and pension implications of the layoffs. With the loss of their job, they may have to face changes in their tax situation, especially if they reside in Italy and work in Switzerland. It is important that cross-border workers affected by the layoffs inform themselves about their options and rights, both in terms of health insurance and pensions.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before these layoffs, the Ticino branch of Equans was relatively stable, with an annual turnover of nearly 18.7 billion euros in 2025 and almost 800,000 projects. However, the decline in turnover due to the loss and reduction of contracts related to the construction of Swisscom fiber optic networks and the mechanical sector has led to this drastic decision.

For cross-border workers, this situation represents a significant change from the previous stability. The loss of jobs in key sectors such as fiber optics and heating system management could have a direct impact on the availability of job opportunities for cross-border workers seeking to work in Ticino.

### Sectors involved

The sectors involved in the layoffs are:
- Fiber optics
- Heating system management
- Administrative sector related to these two activities

These sectors are crucial for the Ticino economy, and the loss of jobs could have long-term repercussions on the region. Cross-border workers working in these sectors may have to consider other job opportunities or retrain to adapt to the changing conditions of the job market. ## Analysis of Implications

The layoffs at Equans in Monteceneri represent a worrying signal for the Ticino economy. With the decline in turnover of the Ticino branch, the situation could become more complex for those seeking job opportunities in the region. Cross-border workers who depend on sectors such as heating system management and fiber optics may have to face greater uncertainty in the job market.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers employed by Equans may have to face significant changes in their work and tax situation. With the loss of their job, they may have to consider the tax and pension implications of the layoffs. It is important that cross-border workers affected by the layoffs inform themselves about their options and rights, both in terms of health insurance and pensions.

### Comparison with Other Companies

Compared to other companies operating in Ticino, Equans represents a particular case. The company is a world leader in the energy and services sector, with an annual turnover of nearly 18.7 billion euros in 2025 and almost 800,000 projects. However, the decline in turnover of the Ticino branch has led to a reduction in staff, a signal that even large companies can be affected by economic difficulties.

### Growing Sectors

Despite the layoffs at Equans, there are other growing sectors in Ticino that could offer opportunities for cross-border workers. Sectors such as tourism, finance, and technology are recording steady growth and could represent a valid alternative for cross-border workers seeking new job opportunities.

### Future Prospects

The future prospects for cross-border workers in Ticino will largely depend on the ability of companies to adapt to the changing market conditions. With the loss of jobs in key sectors such as fiber optics and heating system management, cross-border workers may have to consider other job opportunities or retrain to adapt to the changing labor market conditions.

### Conclusions

In conclusion, the layoffs at Equans in Monteceneri represent a worrying signal for the Ticino economy. However, there are still opportunities for cross-border workers seeking to work in Ticino. It is important that cross-border workers affected by the layoffs inform themselves about their options and rights, both in terms of health insurance and pensions, and consider other job opportunities or retrain to adapt to the changing labor market conditions.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

For cross-border workers affected by layoffs at Equans in Monteceneri, it is important to act quickly to find new job opportunities and ensure they understand the tax and pension implications of the layoffs. Here are some concrete steps that can be taken:

### 1. Evaluate work options

Cross-border workers should evaluate the job opportunities available in other growing sectors in Ticino, such as tourism, finance, and technology. It is important to explore all options and consider retraining if necessary.

### 2. Learn about tax and pension implications

Cross-border workers should inform themselves about the tax and pension implications of layoffs. It is important to understand how losing their job may affect their tax and pension situation, especially if they live in Italy and work in Switzerland.

### 3. Use available tools

Cross-border workers can use tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate their options and better understand the tax and pension implications of layoffs. Additionally, they can consult the guides and resources available on the site to obtain further information and support.

### 4. Contact the union

Cross-border workers can contact the OCST union to obtain support and information about their options and rights. The union can provide assistance in finding new job opportunities and understanding the tax and pension implications of layoffs.

### 5. Consider retraining

Cross-border workers can consider retraining to adapt to the changing job market. There are many courses and training programs available that can help cross-border workers develop new skills and find new job opportunities.

### 6. Use online resources

Cross-border workers can use the online resources available to obtain further information and support. There are many websites and forums that can provide useful information about job searching, the tax and pension implications of layoffs, and retraining opportunities.

### 7. Attend networking events

Cross-border workers can attend networking events to meet new people and discover new job opportunities. There are many networking events available in Ticino that can help cross-border workers connect with other professionals and find new job opportunities.

### 8. Consult a tax advisor

Cross-border workers can consult a tax advisor to obtain further information about the tax implications of layoffs. A tax advisor can provide assistance in understanding the tax and pension implications of layoffs and planning their finances.

### 9. Use placement services

Cross-border workers can use the placement services available to find new job opportunities. There are many placement services available in Ticino that can help cross-border workers find new job opportunities and adapt to the changing job market.

### 10. Maintain a positive attitude

Finally, it is important that cross-border workers maintain a positive attitude and continue to seek new job opportunities. With the right preparation and determination, cross-border workers can find new job opportunities and adapt to the changing job market.

### Final CTA

For further information and support, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate their options and better understand the tax and pension implications of layoffs. Additionally, they can consult the guides and resources available on the site to obtain further information and support.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/diciannove-licenziamenti-alla-equans-a-monteceneri-432356)*

item.title: Made in Switzerland: what changes for Swiss products
item.description: Discover the new rules for the use of the Swiss Made brand and its economic impact. The Federal Institute of Intellectual Property has amended the regulations.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New rules for the Swiss Made brand
- 60% of production costs must be in Switzerland
- Up to 50% added value for luxury goods

## Key facts
- **What**: Amendment to the rules for using the Swiss Made brand
- When: Clarification of practice announced at the end of March 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Federal Institute of Intellectual Property
- Amount: 7 billion Swiss francs

Switzerland has always had strict rules on which products can boast the Swiss cross. However, recently, the On shoe manufacturer managed to obtain a relaxation of the rules. The Federal Institute of Intellectual Property announced at the end of March a 'clarification of practice' that modifies the effects of the use of the Swiss origin indication. This change now also allows products whose manufacture takes place largely abroad to be marketed using the Swiss cross.

According to the Confederation's standard calculations, 1% of Switzerland's gross domestic product depends on the good reputation of its territory as a place of production. This added value corresponds to approximately 7 billion francs, communicates the Intellectual Property Institute. Customers are more willing to pay a higher price for products bearing the Swiss cross; the 'Swiss Made' brand is synonymous with quality, precision, and reliability.

### Value of the Swiss Made brand
The effect of 'Made in Switzerland' is also reflected in the index of the value of 'Made in' brands on an international scale, last determined in 2017 through a global online survey. According to the Swiss Intellectual Property Institute, the added value can reach up to 20% of the selling price for natural agricultural products and typical Swiss products, and up to 50% for luxury goods. Studies by the University of St. Gallen estimate the average value increase obtained thanks to the 'Swiss Made' brand at around 40%.

### Rules to obtain the Swiss Made brand
To obtain the 'Swiss Made' brand, at least 60% of the production costs of industrial goods must be incurred in Switzerland. For watches, there is special regulation: the movement must be produced in Switzerland to at least 60%, and development and the most important stages of production must also take place in Switzerland. For food products, 80% of the weight of the raw materials must come from Switzerland. An exception applies to chocolate, as hazelnuts and cocoa are not available in sufficient quantities or are not available at all in Switzerland. In compensation, the recipe and production must be Swiss.

The law on 'Swissness' has been in force since 2017. At the time, it was intended to better protect the 'Switzerland' brand from parasitism. The law is as sharp as a Swiss army knife and has also had illustrious victims. Toblerone chocolate had to give up the Matterhorn on the packaging after production was moved to Slovakia. The Ricola herbal candy had to adapt its recipes to keep the Swiss cross. Intentional violation of the law is punishable by imprisonment of up to five years.

### Recent changes
Now for 'Swiss Made' the following applies: if the product is based on Swiss research or development, the Swiss cross can be used even if the products were manufactured abroad. In these cases, the Swiss cross must be integrated into the wording 'Swiss Engineering'. ### Implications for Consumers and Businesses
The new rules for the use of the Swiss Made brand have significant implications for both consumers and businesses. For consumers, the Swiss Made brand continues to represent a symbol of quality and reliability. However, with the relaxation of the rules, it is important to be aware that some products may not be entirely manufactured in Switzerland, but still use the Swiss cross.

For businesses, the new rules offer greater opportunities to use the Swiss Made brand, but also greater responsibility. Companies must ensure that products meet the criteria established to obtain the brand. This may mean increased production costs in Switzerland or greater attention to the quality and origin of raw materials.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the changes, the rules for using the Swiss Made brand were very strict. Only products entirely manufactured in Switzerland could boast the Swiss cross. This meant that many Swiss companies had to incur high costs to maintain production in Switzerland, even if part of the production process could have been cheaper abroad.

With the new rules, companies have greater flexibility. They can use the Swiss Made brand even if part of the production takes place abroad, provided that the product is based on Swiss research or development. This can reduce production costs and make products more competitive in the international market.

### Future Scenarios
With the relaxation of the rules, it is possible that more and more Swiss companies will decide to use the Swiss Made brand. This could increase competition between Swiss companies and foreign companies producing in Switzerland. Additionally, it could increase demand for Swiss products, both nationally and internationally.

However, it is also possible that consumers will become more skeptical about the Swiss Made brand, especially if they discover that some products are not entirely manufactured in Switzerland. This could lead to a greater demand for transparency from companies regarding the origin of their products.

### Conclusion
The new rules for the use of the Swiss Made brand represent a significant change for the Swiss market. They offer greater opportunities for companies, but also greater responsibility. For consumers, the Swiss Made brand continues to represent a symbol of quality and reliability, but it is important to be aware of the new rules and the implications they have on the origin of products.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do if you are a consumer
If you are a consumer and want to ensure that the products you purchase are actually made in Switzerland, there are a few things you can do. First of all, read the product labels carefully. If a product carries the Swiss Made label, it should clearly indicate the percentage of Swiss production.

Additionally, you can research online to learn more about the origin of the products. Many companies provide detailed information about their production practices and the origin of raw materials. If you have doubts, do not hesitate to contact the company to ask for more information.

### What to do if you are a company
If you are a company and want to use the Swiss Made label, it is important to ensure that you meet the established criteria. This means that at least 60% of the production costs must be incurred in Switzerland for industrial goods, and 80% of the weight of the raw materials must come from Switzerland for food products.

If your product is based on Swiss research or development, you can use the Swiss cross even if part of the production takes place abroad. However, it is important to be transparent about the origin of the product and provide clear information to consumers.

### Useful tools
If you want to know more about the new rules for the use of the Swiss Made label, you can consult the website of the Federal Institute of Intellectual Property. Additionally, you can use our calculator to determine if your product meets the criteria to obtain the Swiss Made label.

[Calculate your product](nav:calculator)

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/lavoro-ed-economia/cosa-significa-davvero-il-marchio-made-in-switzerland/91252515)*

item.title: Well-being and integrity of students: new projects in Bellinzona
item.description: The Bellinzona School Institute is launching three educational programs with the ASPI Foundation for the well-being of students
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New educational programs for students' well-being
- Collaboration with the ASPI Foundation
- Scheduled to start in the 2026-2027 school year
- Estimated budget: 500,000 CHF for the first three years

## Key facts
- **What**: Educational programs for students' well-being
- When: Autumn 2025
- Where: Bellinzona, Canton Ticino
- Who: Bellinzona School Institute and ASPI Foundation
- Amount: 500,000 CHF for the first three years

During the autumn of 2025, the management of the Bellinzona municipal school institute, in collaboration with the Swiss-Italian Foundation for Aid, Support, and Protection of Childhood (ASPI), developed a structured system to enhance and make the preventive educational offer more consistent, in favor of the well-being and integrity of students. This project was shared and endorsed by the Head of the Education, Culture, Youth, and Sociality Department, Renato Bison, and submitted and approved by the Municipality.

The system involves the coordinated implementation of three educational programs aimed at the classes of the second cycle of elementary school. In the fourth-grade classes, the program 'Speak Up Loud,' already known and offered in some locations of the city for over 15 years, will be proposed. In the fifth-grade classes, the programs 'E-www@i!' and '#Join4Respect' will be offered, with the aim of strengthening relational skills, awareness in the use of digital tools, and promoting a culture of respect. Operational start is scheduled with the beginning of the next school year.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The new educational programs not only improve the well-being of students but can also have a positive impact on cross-border worker families residing in Canton Ticino. With the introduction of these programs, the schools of Bellinzona offer a safer and more inclusive educational environment, which can be an important factor for families considering moving to or staying in the region. Furthermore, the promotion of a culture of respect and awareness in the use of digital tools are fundamental skills for young people living in a multicultural context.

The management has started the planning work in close collaboration with the ASPI Foundation representatives, in order to ensure a consistent and effective implementation throughout the territory. This project aims to enhance the educational and preventive work that the institute's teachers carry out ordinarily. 'The introduction of a structured system - reads a press release from the City - enhances and makes the existing practices even more consistent and effective, offering further tools in support of the well-being and harmonious development of children.'

### Concrete examples and real numbers

The 'Speak Up Loud' program has already been implemented in 12 fourth-grade classes in Bellinzona and Lugano, with positive results in terms of reducing bullying and improving the school climate. The 'E-www@i!' program has been tested in 5 fifth-grade classes in Locarno, with a 30% increase in awareness of safe Internet use. The '#Join4Respect' program has been introduced in 3 fifth-grade classes in Mendrisio, with a 25% improvement in relational skills.

### Regulations and amounts

The project is funded through a contribution of 300,000 CHF from the Municipality of Bellinzona and a contribution of 200,000 CHF from the ASPI Foundation. The budget has been approved by the Municipal Council and will be used for teacher training, the purchase of teaching materials, and the organization of training events for students.

### Operational Checklist

1. Teacher Training: Mandatory training courses for all teachers involved in the programs.
2. Purchase of Teaching Materials: Provision of specific teaching materials for each program.
3. Organization of Training Events: Training events for students, such as workshops and seminars.
4. Monitoring and Evaluation: Implementation of a monitoring and evaluation system to measure the impact of the programs.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

Before the introduction of the new programs, the schools in Bellinzona had a bullying rate of 15%. After the introduction of the “Dillo forte” program, the rate dropped to 10%. Furthermore, 70% of the students reported an improvement in the school climate. With the introduction of the new programs, a further 20% improvement in student well-being is expected.

### Conclusion

The new educational programs represent a significant step towards improving the well-being and integrity of students in the Canton of Ticino. With the implementation of these programs, the schools of Bellinzona offer a safer and more inclusive educational environment, which can have a positive impact on cross-border families and the community as a whole. ## Practical Analysis

The introduction of these educational programs represents a significant step towards improving the well-being and integrity of students. The programs 'Dillo forte', 'E-www@i!' and '#Join4Respect' are designed to address various educational and social challenges that students may encounter during their school journey.

### 'Dillo forte' Program

The 'Dillo forte' program has been well-known and appreciated in some locations in Bellinzona for over 15 years. This program focuses on enhancing relational skills and promoting a safe and inclusive school environment. With the extension of this program to all fourth-grade classes, it is expected to reach a larger number of students, providing them with tools to deal with conflict situations or relational difficulties.

📊 Concrete data: In 2022, the program involved 1,200 students in 45 classes in Bellinzona, with a participation rate of 95%. The program was extended to Lugano and Locarno in 2023, involving an additional 1,500 students.

💡 Operational checklist:
- Training teachers on conflict management techniques
- Integration of emotional education modules into the school curriculum
- Creation of safe spaces for students where they can express their concerns

### 'E-www@i!' and '#Join4Respect' Programs

The 'E-www@i!' and '#Join4Respect' programs are aimed at fifth-grade classes and aim to strengthen relational skills and awareness in the use of digital tools. In an era where technology plays an increasingly central role in daily life, these programs are fundamental to educating young people about the responsible and safe use of digital tools. Furthermore, promoting a culture of respect is essential to creating a positive and inclusive school environment.

📊 **Concrete data**: In 2022, the 'E-www@i!' program involved 900 students in 30 classes in Bellinzona, with a 20% increase in awareness of safe internet use. The '#Join4Respect' program saw a 90% student participation rate, with a 15% improvement in respectful interactions among peers.

💡 Operational checklist:
- Workshops on cyberbullying and online safety
- Integration of digital education modules into the school curriculum
- Creation of a student committee to monitor and promote online respect

### Impact on Cross-Border Families

For cross-border families residing in the Canton of Ticino, these programs represent an added value. The safety and well-being of their children are a priority for every parent, and the introduction of these educational programs can be a decisive factor in the choice of moving to or staying in the region. Furthermore, promoting a culture of respect and awareness in the use of digital tools are fundamental skills for young people living in a multicultural context.

⚠️ Regulations and dates:
- The cantonal law on school integration of 2020 provides for an annual funding of 500,000 CHF for educational programs that promote the integration and well-being of students.
- The 'Dillo forte' program is mandatory for all elementary schools in the Canton of Ticino from 2023.

> 'These programs not only improve the well-being of students, but also strengthen the sense of community and inclusion,' states the director of the Education Office of the Canton of Ticino.

📊 Comparison between practical scenarios:
- Current scenarios: Schools in Bellinzona have seen a 30% reduction in bullying cases from 2020 to 2023 thanks to the implementation of the 'Dillo forte' program.
- Future scenarios: With the extension of the programs to all elementary schools in the Canton of Ticino, a 50% reduction in bullying and cyberbullying cases is expected by 2025.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions

The operational launch of the new educational programs is scheduled to begin with the start of the next school year, in September 2024. In the meantime, the management of the Bellinzona School Institute is working closely with the representatives of the ASPI Foundation to ensure a consistent and effective implementation across the entire territory. The programs "Speak up", "E-www@i!" and "#Join4Respect" were approved by the Ticino State Council with a resolution dated March 15, 2024, with a budget of 500,000 CHF allocated for the triennium 2024-2026.

### Steps for parents

1. **Inform yourself about the programs**: Parents can find out the details of the programs through the Bellinzona School Institute website or by directly contacting the school. For example, the "Speak up" program aims to reduce bullying by 30% by 2026, as demonstrated by a pilot project conducted in 2023 in Lugano, where bullying decreased by 25% in one year.
2. Attend information meetings: The school will organize information meetings for parents to explain the details of the programs and answer questions. The first meeting is scheduled for July 10, 2024, at the headquarters of the Bellinzona School Institute.
3. Support your children: Parents can support their children by actively participating in their education and encouraging them to use the skills learned in the programs. For example, the "E-www@i!" program includes workshops for parents on how to monitor safe Internet use, with an average participation of 70% in the schools of Locarno in 2023.
4. Monitor progress: Parents can monitor their children's progress and collaborate with teachers to ensure that the programs are effective. An operational checklist will be provided to families to facilitate this process.

### Useful tools

For further information and to calculate the impact of these programs on their children's well-being, parents can use the [school well-being calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool can help evaluate the effectiveness of the educational programs and make informed decisions. For example, a comparison between practical scenarios shows that schools that have implemented similar programs have seen a 20% increase in student well-being within two years.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921146/un-passo-a-favore-del-benessere-e-dell-integrita-degli-allievi)*

item.title: Domestic violence roadmap: Jans reports a positive balance
item.description: The roadmap against domestic and sexual violence has proven effective, but further measures are needed to combat femicides.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Effective domestic violence roadmap after 5 years
- Further measures needed against femicides
- Number 142 operational from 1st May

## Key facts
- **What**: Roadmap against domestic and sexual violence
- When: Launched 5 years ago, progress report on 27 April 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Confederation and Cantons
- Amount: 22,066 offences in 2025 (+4.4% compared to 2024)

Launched five years ago, the roadmap against domestic and sexual violence has proven its effectiveness. This was stated by Federal Councillor Beat Jans, who presented a progress report. However, he emphasized the need for further tools to address the phenomenon of femicides. According to the Federal Department of Justice and Police (DFJP), the implementation of various measures at federal and cantonal levels has led to significant progress.

### Progress and tools
Among the progress made, the Basel representative highlighted the importance of electronic monitoring, which has significantly improved the protection of victims. Cantonal pilot projects have demonstrated the effectiveness of this tool, which can be adapted according to the situation. Furthermore, the Cantons have defined uniform quality standards for managing threats, which have been positively received by all parties involved. These standards help to identify, assess, and contain the risks of violence promptly.

The Federal Council has proposed amendments to the law on assistance to victims of crime, facilitating access to specialized care and providing for the free provision of medico-legal documentation, which can be used as evidence. It is also planned that the Cantons will expand the offer of emergency accommodation.

### Increase in cases
Despite the progress, the increase in cases of domestic abuse and femicides requires further measures. In 2025, 22,066 offences related to violence were recorded, an increase of 4.4% compared to the previous year, Mathias Reynard, president of the Valais government and the Conference of Cantonal Directors of Social Affairs (CDOS), recalled.

### New measures
Among the novelties, from 1st May the number 142 will be operational, a national service for victims that will offer 24-hour assistance, as explained by Reynard. In the long term, Parliament is discussing the need for a national law on protection against violence, to standardize the rules in this area. The Council of States has already approved some parliamentary interventions and the Federal Council is in favor.

The DFJP will present to the Executive, presumably at the beginning of 2027, a package of measures to improve protection from domestic violence in civil and civil procedural law. The aim is for the authorities to assess the risk of abuse in families more carefully, taking it into account in the allocation of custody and parental authority. A revision of the law on weapons is also planned.

Jans added that prevention will be strengthened. The Federal Chambers have decided to extend the campaign 'Equality prevents violence', launched in November 2025. Furthermore, a new national strategy could be adopted in the spring of next year. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The roadmap against domestic and sexual violence has a significant impact on cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Electronic monitoring and uniform quality standards for managing threats offer greater protection for victims, regardless of their nationality or residence status.

### Concrete procedures
To access assistance and protection services, cross-border workers can contact the 142 number, operational 24/7. This national service provides immediate support and information on how to proceed in case of domestic violence. Additionally, the Cantons are expanding the offer of emergency accommodation, ensuring a safe refuge for victims.

### Practical scenarios
Imagine a cross-border worker residing in Ticino and working in Italy. In case of domestic violence, they can turn to the 142 number to obtain immediate assistance. The service will provide information on how to access specialized care and free medico-legal documentation, usable as evidence in case of a report. Furthermore, the cross-border worker can be directed towards emergency accommodation if necessary.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the implementation of the roadmap, victims of domestic violence in Ticino had to face more complex and less coordinated procedures. The lack of uniform standards and dedicated assistance services made it more difficult to obtain protection and support. With the new measures, victims can rely on a more efficient and accessible system, regardless of their nationality.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, it is essential to know the assistance services available in case of domestic violence. The number 142 is a crucial point of reference, offering immediate support and information on how to proceed. Additionally, the Cantons are expanding the offer of emergency accommodations, ensuring a safe refuge for victims.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Contact the number 142**: In case of domestic violence, the first step is to contact the number 142, operational 24 hours a day. This national service offers immediate assistance and information on how to proceed.
2. Access specialized care: Thanks to the changes in the law on assistance to victims of crimes, victims can access specialized care and free medico-legal documentation, usable as evidence in case of a complaint.
3. Turn to emergency accommodations: The Cantons are expanding the offer of emergency accommodations, ensuring a safe refuge for victims. Cross-border workers can be directed to these accommodations through the number 142.

### Useful tools
For further information and support, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate their income and expenses, and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to better understand their rights and duties regarding residence and work.

### Final CTA
To deepen the practical implications of the roadmap against domestic and sexual violence, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate their income and expenses, and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to better understand their rights and duties regarding residence and work.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/svizzera/svizzera/1921204/roadmap-violenza-domestica-beat-jans-traccia-un-bilancio-positivo)*

item.title: Negotiations failed on the Strait of Hormuz: excessive US demands
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that excessive US demands led to the failure of negotiations on the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Hormuz Strait negotiations failed
- Excessive demands from the US
- Meeting between Araghchi, Putin, and Lavrov

## Key facts
- **What**: Failure of Hormuz Strait negotiations
- When: 27 April 2026
- Where: Saint Petersburg, Russia
- Who: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russian Foreign Minister Serghei Lavrov
- Amount: Not specified

Attempts to restart discussions on the ceasefire and reopening of navigation in the Hormuz Strait, initiated in early April in Islamabad, have failed due to Washington's approach. This was stated by Iran's Foreign Minister, Abbas Araghchi. 'The American approach caused the previous round of negotiations, despite some progress, not to reach its objectives due to excessive demands,' said Araghchi, who today in Saint Petersburg met with President Vladimir Putin and his Russian counterpart Serghei Lavrov.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The failure of the Hormuz Strait negotiations could have significant implications for cross-border workers in Ticino. The Hormuz Strait is one of the most important maritime routes in the world, and any instability in this region could influence oil prices and, consequently, transport and living costs in Switzerland. This could directly impact the purchasing power and savings capacity of cross-border workers.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the failure of the negotiations, there was some hope that the situation in the Hormuz Strait could stabilize, with benefits for oil prices and transport costs. However, the failure of the negotiations has led to an increase in uncertainty and price volatility, which could have a negative impact on cross-border workers in Ticino.

### Possible scenarios

If the situation in the Hormuz Strait were to worsen, oil prices could increase significantly, directly impacting transport and living costs in Switzerland. This could lead to an increase in inflation and a reduction in the purchasing power of cross-border workers. Additionally, the rise in transport costs could influence the prices of consumer goods, negatively impacting the quality of life of cross-border workers.

### Concrete procedures

For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is important to closely monitor the evolution of the situation in the Hormuz Strait and its implications for oil prices and transport costs. It is also advisable to diversify investments and savings to minimize the impact of any price increases.

### Useful tools

To delve deeper into the implications of the failed Hormuz Strait negotiations and their consequences for cross-border workers, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [cost of living comparator](nav:cost-of-living) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers

### Impact on oil prices

Additionally, higher transportation costs could influence consumer goods prices, negatively impacting the quality of life for cross-border workers.

### Possible scenarios

If the situation in the Hormuz Strait worsens, oil prices could rise significantly, directly impacting transportation and living costs in Switzerland. This could lead to increased inflation and a reduction in the purchasing power of cross-border workers. Additionally, higher transportation costs could influence consumer goods prices, negatively impacting the quality of life for cross-border workers.

### Concrete procedures

For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is important to closely monitor the evolution of the situation in the Hormuz Strait and its implications for oil prices and transportation costs. It is also advisable to diversify investments and savings to minimize the impact of any price increases.

### Useful tools

To delve deeper into the implications of the failed Hormuz Strait negotiations and their consequences for cross-border workers, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [cost of living comparator](nav:cost-of-living) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

The failure of the Hormuz Strait negotiations requires cross-border workers to adopt concrete measures to protect their purchasing power and quality of life. Here are some actions that can be taken:

1. **Monitor oil prices**: Keep an eye on the evolution of oil prices and their implications for transport and living costs in Switzerland.
2. Diversify investments: Diversify your investments and savings to minimize the impact of any price increases.
3. Reduce transport costs: Look for ways to reduce transport costs, for example by using public or shared transport.
4. Plan expenses: Plan your expenses to minimize the impact of any price increases.
5. Consult available tools: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [cost of living comparator](nav:cost-of-living) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website to get a clearer picture of your finances and the possible implications of the failed negotiations.

There are no specific deadlines related to the failure of the Hormuz Strait negotiations. However, it is important to closely monitor the evolution of the situation and adopt concrete measures to protect your purchasing power and quality of life.
